title: Layer 2A - Fetch sub-port contract
description: URL-in / raw-content-out. The first of Layer 2A's two decoupled sub-ports (the other is extract.schema.json). FETCH escalates by tier and never extracts or truncates; it hands raw HTML to EXTRACT. It enforces an http(s) scheme allowlist and refuses private/reserved egress targets unless allow_private_hosts is set, and follows redirects with the same guard on each hop. The default closure is permissive (httpx, curl_cffi); browser and stealth tiers (Crawl4AI, nodriver, Camoufox) and the jina_reader fallback are opt-in adapters named in the enums but not part of the base install. Proxy egress is owned by Layer 1; FETCH only consumes an endpoint, never holds credentials.

Proxy.description: An egress endpoint supplied by Layer 1. Proxy-agnostic: the same shape carries a direct proxy, a VPN socks5 sidecar, or a paid residential pool.

per_host_delay_ms.description: Minimum spacing between requests to the same host.

respect_robots.description: Honor robots.txt for the fetched URL. Default false: a single deliberate fetch of an agent-chosen URL is closer to a browser open than to crawling. Enable for crawl-like batch use.

tier_hint.description: auto = start cheap and escalate only on a detected block. http = plain/impersonated HTTP only (no browser). browser/stealth select the opt-in headless tiers.

render_js.description: Force JS rendering (browser tier). null = decide by tier_hint.

wait_for.description: CSS selector or text to await before capture (browser tiers only).

timeout_ms.description: Per-operation (connect/read) timeout, not a total wall-clock budget.

max_bytes.description: Transport guard ONLY: stop downloading a response body past this many bytes (defends against multi-GB transfers); defaults to 10 MB. This is NOT an output/LLM length cap; extracted content is never truncated. null removes the guard.

allow_private_hosts.description: Permit fetching private/loopback/link-local/reserved addresses (e.g. an internal service). Default false: the SSRF guard refuses them, including the 169.254.169.254 cloud-metadata endpoint.

status.description: HTTP status; 0 when no response was received.

ok.description: A usable response body was retrieved (independent of blocked).

raw_html.description: Unmodified response body (never truncated for content reasons).

blocked.description: An anti-bot challenge/interstitial was detected (may still carry partial content).

block_reason.description: Branchable cause when blocked. Recommended vocabulary (kept as a string so new vendors do not force a contract bump): cloudflare_challenge, cloudflare_firewall, datadome, perimeterx, akamai, imperva, ddos_guard, rate_limited, forbidden_suspected_bot, unavailable_suspected_block, auth_required, legal_geo_block.

tier_attempts.description: Adapters tried in order before this result (escalation trail).

error.description: Transport error when no response was obtained.

failure_kind.description: Structured cause for a failed fetch (status 0), letting a consumer set retriability precisely instead of parsing the error text. Null on success.
